sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Ulta Stock Drops Despite Q4 Earnings Beat: What To Know
feed.summary: Ulta Beauty (NASDAQ: ULTA) reported a mixed reaction to its fourth-quarter earnings despite beating analyst estimates for both earnings per share and revenue. The company posted strong sales growth year-over-year and provided an outlook for fiscal year 2026 that generally topped analyst expectations. Despite the positive results and outlook, ULTA stock experienced an 8.63% drop in extended trading.

feed.title: AT&T Completes CAD-Denominated Long-Term Debt Offering
feed.summary: AT&T has successfully completed a CAD-denominated long-term debt offering, issuing CAD$2.25 billion in global notes due in 2036 and 2056. This move utilizes an existing SEC shelf registration to support its long-term funding strategy. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates AT&T (T) as Neutral, citing solid financial performance balanced by high leverage and variable free cash flow, with a bullish technical trend but overextended momentum.

feed.title: Ulta Beauty Expects Growth to Slow This Fiscal Year
feed.summary: Ulta Beauty anticipates a slowdown in growth for the current fiscal year, projecting 6% to 7% sales growth compared to 9.7% in the previous year. The cosmetics retailer also provided an earnings per share forecast of $28.05 to $28.55 and comparable sales growth of 2.5% to 3.5%. This guidance comes despite reporting higher revenue in its latest quarter, though profit was lower.

feed.title: Bank of America Securities’ head of ABS research O’Neill to join Auto Finance Capital Summit
feed.summary: Theresa O’Neill, managing director and head of ABS strategy at Bank of America Securities, will present on consumer credit at the inaugural Auto Finance Capital Summit. Her presentation, "Affordability, Inflation and the Consumer Credit Divide," will cover generational borrower risk, income, affordability, delinquencies, and the capital impact of loan terms. Bank of America was the 10th-largest auto lender in 2024, with auto outstandings of $55.3 billion in Q4 2025.

feed.title: Shantanu Narayen Announces Decision to Transition as Adobe’s CEO Once Successor is Named
feed.summary: Shantanu Narayen, after 18 years as CEO of Adobe, has announced his decision to transition from his role once a successor is appointed. He will remain as Chair of the Board. The Board of Directors has initiated a search for a new CEO, with Lead Independent Director Frank Calderoni heading the committee to consider both internal and external candidates.

feed.title: O’Reilly Auto Raises $850 Million in New Notes
feed.summary: O’Reilly Automotive (ORLY) has issued $850 million in 5.100% senior unsecured notes due March 12, 2036, providing long-term funding for the company with standard creditor protections. An analyst rating on ORLY stock is currently a Buy with a $109.00 price target, though Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, gives ORLY a Neutral rating due to strong operating profitability and positive cash generation balanced by significant balance sheet leverage and rich valuation. ORLY is a retailer and distributor of automotive aftermarket parts with a current market cap of $79.14 billion.

feed.title: Salesforce (NYSE: CRM) issues $25B notes to fund $25B buyback
feed.summary: Salesforce has announced a plan to repurchase $25 billion of its common stock through accelerated share repurchase agreements, with initial shares expected by March 16, 2026, and final settlement in Q4 2026. The buyback will be funded by issuing $25 billion in senior notes across eight tranches maturing between 2028 and 2066, expected to generate approximately $24.885 billion in net proceeds. Additionally, Salesforce entered into a new $6 billion five-year senior unsecured term loan agreement to refinance existing debt.

feed.title: PNC appoints first chief hospitality officer amid accelerated branch expansion
feed.summary: PNC Financial Services Group has appointed Chris Hill as its first chief hospitality officer as it pursues an ambitious branch expansion strategy. The Pittsburgh-based bank believes that exceptional customer service, led by this new role, will be a key differentiator. This move comes as PNC aims to expand its physical presence nationwide.

feed.title: BofA Notes Strong Profitability and Sales Performance at The TJX Companies (TJX)
feed.summary: BofA has increased its price target for The TJX Companies (TJX) to $175 from $168, maintaining a Buy rating due to strong sales and profitability performance. TJX reported impressive FY26 results with net sales of $60.4 billion and diluted EPS of $4.87, and plans to increase dividends by 13% and repurchase $2.50–$2.75 billion in stock in FY27. The company, known for brands like T.J. Maxx, Marshalls, and HomeGoods, is expected to continue gaining market share.

feed.title: Global Payments Completes $1 Billion Senior Notes Offering
feed.summary: Global Payments (GPN) has completed a $1 billion public debt offering of senior notes due in 2028 and 2033. The company will use the proceeds to refinance existing debt and reduce borrowings, aiming to extend its maturity profile and strengthen its capital structure. Spark, TipRanks' AI Analyst, rates GPN as Neutral, citing strong profitability and a positive forward outlook, but tempered by weak technical signals and increased leverage.

feed.title: T. ROWE PRICE OHA SELECT PRIVATE CREDIT FUND ANNOUNCES DECEMBER 31, 2025 FINANCIAL RESULTS AND DECLARED TOTAL DISTRIBUTIONS OF $0.69 PER SHARE IN Q4 2025
feed.summary: T. Rowe Price OHA Select Private Credit Fund (OCREDIT) reported its financial results for the quarter ended December 31, 2025, announcing total distributions of $0.69 per share. The fund experienced net portfolio growth of nearly $210.1 million, expanding its investment portfolio to $2.9 billion across 135 companies in 22 sectors with a weighted average portfolio yield of 10.0%. OCREDIT emphasized disciplined underwriting and risk management in a volatile market, continuing to grow its assets and shareholder base through the issuance of various common share classes.

feed.title: Colgate-Palmolive Announces Quarterly Dividend Increase and Elects Christopher Boerner, Ph.D. to Board of Directors
feed.summary: Colgate-Palmolive (NYSE:CL) announced a quarterly common stock cash dividend increase to $0.53 per share, effective in the second quarter of 2026, marking uninterrupted dividends since 1895. The company also elected Christopher Boerner, Ph.D., Board Chair and CEO of Bristol-Myers Squibb, to its Board of Directors, effective March 15, 2026. Steven A. Cahillane will not seek re-election to the Board at the upcoming Annual Meeting of Stockholders.

feed.title: Otis CFO Cristina Méndez to join J.P. Morgan Industrials fireside chat
feed.summary: Otis (NYSE: OTIS) announced an updated timing for its CFO, Cristina Méndez, to participate in a fireside chat at the J.P. Morgan Industrials Conference on Wednesday, March 18, 2026, at 1:35 p.m. ET. The event will be broadcast live on Otis's website. This update clarifies the scheduling of the event and supersedes any previously stated times.

feed.title: CPKC certifies 14 new Site Ready locations across North America
feed.summary: CPKC has certified 14 new "Site Ready" industrial development locations across North America, expanding its "Room to Grow" strategy. These sites, totaling over 6,600 acres in six U.S. states, three Canadian provinces, and two Mexican states, offer direct rail access and aim to accelerate commercial growth for manufacturers and logistics operators. The program emphasizes efficient industrial solutions, quicker development timelines, and significant economic impact for businesses and communities.

feed.title: CPKC opens 6,600 acres of rail-ready land for manufacturers across North America
feed.summary: Canadian Pacific Kansas City (CPKC) has certified 14 new "Site Ready" industrial locations across North America, adding over 6,600 acres of developable land for manufacturers and logistics operators. This expansion brings the total number of certified sites to 22, spanning six U.S. states, three Canadian provinces, and two Mexican states. The program, developed in partnership with Burns & McDonnell, aims to streamline development, accelerate timelines, and boost economic growth through rail connectivity.

feed.title: Assessing Custom Truck One Source (CTOS) Valuation After Record Q4 2025 Results And 2026 Outlook
feed.summary: Custom Truck One Source (CTOS) reported record Q4 2025 revenue of US$528.18 million, exceeding profit and adjusted EBITDA expectations. Despite recent share price choppiness, the stock is considered undervalued by Simply Wall St's narrative, which pegs fair value at $7.75 per share compared to its last close of $6.01. This valuation is supported by sustained demand from grid modernization, expanding rental fleets, and high utilization rates, though risks such as backlog stabilization and funding costs need to be considered.

feed.title: Assessing Extra Space Storage (EXR) Valuation After Mixed Recent Share Price Performance
feed.summary: Extra Space Storage (EXR) has experienced mixed share price performance recently but is currently trading at US$141.52 with an indicated 16% intrinsic discount compared to a fair value of $150.95. The valuation narrative highlights growing ancillary income streams and an expanding third-party management platform, leveraging demand from small businesses, but also acknowledges risks such as property tax expense growth and potential storage oversupply. Investors are encouraged to consider the detailed analysis, including risks and rewards, for a comprehensive view.

feed.title: Citi Expects Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (WSM) Q4 Earnings to Top Market Estimates
feed.summary: Citi has raised its price target for Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (WSM) to $208 from $188, maintaining a Neutral rating, and forecasts the company's Q4 earnings to exceed market expectations. Additionally, Williams-Sonoma's brand GreenRow launched its first physical store in New York City, marking a shift to a multichannel retail strategy. This new location focuses on sustainable and ethically sourced home furnishings, aligning with the brand's commitment to design, quality, and environmental responsibility.

feed.title: Ulta: Fiscal Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Ulta Beauty Inc. reported fiscal fourth-quarter earnings of $356.7 million, or $8.01 per share, meeting Wall Street expectations. The beauty retailer exceeded revenue forecasts by posting $3.9 billion for the quarter. For the full year, Ulta recorded profits of $1.15 billion on revenues of $12.39 billion, and anticipates full-year earnings between $28.05 and $28.55 per share.

feed.title: Calavo’s (NASDAQ:CVGW) Q4 CY2025: Strong Sales
feed.summary: Calavo Growers (NASDAQ:CVGW) reported Q4 CY2025 results with revenue of $122.2 million, surpassing analyst estimates but representing a 20.8% year-on-year decline. The company's non-GAAP profit per share of $0.27 also exceeded analysts' consensus. Despite challenges in revenue growth over recent years, Calavo showed sequential improvement in its Fresh and Prepared segments during the quarter.

feed.title: Customer Narrative on Hard Drives Bodes Well for Western Digital (WDC)
feed.summary: Western Digital Corp. (NASDAQ: WDC) is positioned favorably as customers increasingly view hard drives as strategic components for AI and cloud infrastructure, moving beyond their traditional commodity status. This shift, highlighted by CEO Tiang Yew Tan, emphasizes the company's focus on delivering high-quality, scalable storage solutions. Bank of America Securities has also raised its price target for WDC, reiterating a Buy rating and indicating strong confidence in the company's long-term prospects.

feed.title: Mitchell Wells Fargo drive-up lane set to reopen this summer
feed.summary: Wells Fargo's Mitchell branch is addressing temporary staff shortages that affected service capacity and led to the closure of its drive-up lane. With recent hires, the bank expects the drive-up lane to reopen this summer, reassuring the community of its commitment despite national branch reductions. Customers can currently access full services inside the branch.

feed.title: Champions Oncology reports Q3 revenue of $16.6 million
feed.summary: Champions Oncology (NASDAQ: CSBR) reported a Q3 fiscal 2026 revenue of $16.6 million, a 2.8% decline year-over-year, primarily due to the absence of a large data license transaction from the prior year. Despite a 32% increase in study service revenue, the company posted a net loss of $279,000 and adjusted EBITDA of $574,000, significantly lower than the previous year. Costs also rose, but the company finished the quarter with $7.1 million in cash and no debt.

feed.title: Seagate (STX) Capitalizing on AI Driven Data Storage Demand
feed.summary: Seagate Technology Holdings (STX) is well-positioned to capitalize on the increasing demand for data storage driven by AI advancements. Executive VP & CFO Gianluca Romano highlighted that AI necessitates higher storage levels but doesn't require new hard disk types, aligning with Seagate's existing product mix. Citi reaffirmed its Buy rating on STX, raising the price target to $480, citing "durable" demand for the company's mass-capacity data storage solutions.

feed.title: Omnicom And Interpublic Merger Tests Scale Synergies And AI Ambitions
feed.summary: Omnicom Group (NYSE:OMC) has announced an agreement to acquire Interpublic Group, forming a new global advertising powerhouse. This merger aims to combine Omnicom's creative and analytics strengths with Interpublic's agencies and client network, with investors closely watching its impact on client strategy, digital offerings, and competitive position. The deal also presents challenges related to integration complexity, potential client churn, and the effective leveraging of AI capabilities.

feed.title: A Look At Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY) Valuation After Recent Share Price Volatility
feed.summary: Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMY) has seen recent share price volatility, closing at $59.98. Despite some short-term dips, the stock shows strong 90-day and multi-year returns. According to a narrative by Simply Wall St analyst Evangelos, BMY is considered modestly undervalued with a fair value of $65, suggesting a potential opportunity for investors.

feed.title: Shantanu Narayen Message to Adobe Employees on decision to Transition from CEO Role Once Successor is Named
feed.summary: Shantanu Narayen announced his decision to transition from his role as CEO of Adobe after over 18 years, marking his 100th earnings call with the company. He will work with the Board to identify his successor and ensure a smooth transition, but will remain as Chair of the Board to support the new CEO. Narayen reflected on Adobe's growth, innovation, and leadership in the digital experience and AI eras, expressing confidence in the company's future and its employees.

feed.title: International Paper (NYSE:IP) Hits New 12-Month Low - Here's What Happened
feed.summary: International Paper (NYSE:IP) shares fell to a new 52-week low of $35.45 after reporting a quarterly EPS of ($0.08), missing estimates despite a 53.1% year-over-year revenue increase. Analysts have a "Hold" consensus rating with a $48.25 price target, suggesting potential upside. Notably, CEO Andrew K. Silvernail recently purchased 50,000 shares, and institutional investors own a significant portion of the company's stock.

feed.title: Stock Traders Purchase Large Volume of Call Options on Gilead Sciences (NASDAQ:GILD)
feed.summary: Stock investors purchased a significantly high volume of call options on Gilead Sciences (NASDAQ:GILD), indicating bullish sentiment. This unusual activity occurred despite recent insider selling by executives, including Johanna Mercier and Andrew D. Dickinson. Gilead Sciences has also increased its quarterly dividend and received positive price target adjustments from several research firms.

feed.title: Deutsche Bank Warns Of $30B Risk In Private Credit Holdings
feed.summary: Deutsche Bank has issued a warning in its annual report regarding a $30 billion exposure to private credit, citing concerns about high technology valuations, the sustainability of AI-driven capital spending, and potential impacts on business models and client credit risk. The bank plans to expand its infrastructure investment and private credit offerings despite the risks. Other major financial institutions like Morgan Stanley, Blackstone, and BlackRock have also faced challenges with private credit redemptions.

feed.title: CapsoVision to Release Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Financial Results on Thursday, March 26, 2026
feed.summary: CapsoVision, Inc. (NASDAQ: CV) announced it will release its fourth quarter and full year 2025 financial results on Thursday, March 26, 2026, after the market closes. The company will also host a conference call and live webcast on the same day at 1:30 pm PT / 4:30 pm ET to discuss the results and provide a corporate update. Individuals can access the call via phone or webcast, with a replay available for one year.

feed.title: Calavo Growers, Inc. Announces First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
feed.summary: Calavo Growers, Inc. (Nasdaq-GS: CVGW) reported its first fiscal quarter 2026 financial results, with total net sales of $122.2 million, down from $154.4 million in the prior year. Despite a pressured avocado pricing environment, the Fresh segment saw a 17% increase in avocado carton volume, while the Prepared segment's sales increased by 20% due to new customer acquisition and expanded existing relationships. The company also provided an update on its pending merger with Mission Produce, Inc., expecting to close the transaction in the third fiscal quarter of 2026.

feed.title: Kroger's Board of Directors Declares Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: The Kroger Co.'s Board of Directors declared a quarterly dividend of 35 cents per share, payable on June 1, 2026, to shareholders of record as of May 15, 2026. The company highlights its consistent dividend growth and its capital allocation strategy, which balances investments in the business with returning capital to shareholders, maintaining an investment-grade debt rating. Kroger emphasizes its commitment to driving long-term sustainable net earnings growth while serving millions of customers through its associates.

feed.title: DHI Group to Participate in the Sidoti Small Cap Conference on March 19, 2026
feed.summary: DHI Group, Inc. (NYSE: DHX) announced that its management, including CEO Art Zeile and CFO Greg Schippers, will participate in the virtual Sidoti Small Cap Conference on March 18-19, 2026. They are scheduled to present on March 19th at 12:15 PM ET and will hold one-on-one meetings with investors. A live webcast and replay of their presentation will be available on the company's website.

feed.title: To cool AI-hungry data centers, Eaton buys Boyd Thermal
feed.summary: Eaton (NYSE: ETN) has completed its acquisition of Boyd Thermal from Goldman Sachs Asset Management to enhance its data center cooling solutions. This acquisition, which includes Boyd Thermal's expertise in liquid cooling, aims to address the increasing demand from AI-driven data centers by providing integrated grid-to-chip solutions. Boyd Thermal will be integrated into Eaton's Electrical Global business segment and is expected to be accretive to adjusted earnings within two years.

feed.title: Motorola Solutions Appoints Peter Leav to Board of Directors
feed.summary: Motorola Solutions (NYSE: MSI) has appointed Peter Leav to its board of directors. Leav, a senior advisor at TPG, brings over 25 years of leadership experience in software technology, including previous roles as CEO of McAfee, BMC Software, and Polycom. His appointment is expected to provide valuable expertise in software and cybersecurity to Motorola Solutions as it continues to expand its technology portfolio and mission for public safety.

feed.title: Corvus Pharmaceuticals 10‑K: $0 Revenue, $(0.19) EPS (basic) — net loss $(15.28M)
feed.summary: Corvus Pharmaceuticals reported zero revenue and a net loss of $15.283 million, despite $27.141 million in non-operating income from warrant fair value changes. The operating loss widened to $(42.971) million due to increased R&D spending to advance soquelitinib into Phase 3 and Phase 2 trials. A January 2026 follow-on offering raised approximately $189 million, extending the company’s cash runway into the second quarter of 2028.

feed.title: Linde (NASDAQ:LIN) EVP Sean Durbin Sells 6,520 Shares
feed.summary: Linde's EVP Sean Durbin sold 6,520 shares of the company's stock for over $3.11 million, reducing his position by 44.44%. This transaction follows Linde's strong quarterly earnings, where it beat analyst expectations for EPS and revenue, provided optimistic guidance for 2026, and increased its quarterly dividend. The stock maintains a consensus "Buy" rating from analysts with an average price target of $508.20 and significant institutional ownership.

feed.title: Archer Daniels Midland (NYSE:ADM) SVP Sells $3,424,000.00 in Stock
feed.summary: Archer Daniels Midland (NYSE:ADM) SVP Gregory Morris sold 50,000 shares of the company's stock for $3.424 million on March 10th. This sale reduced his stake by 14.28% to 300,122 shares. The company recently reported better-than-expected quarterly EPS, though revenue declined, and increased its quarterly dividend, while analysts maintain a "Reduce" rating with a $58 target price.

feed.title: Meet DHI Group’s CEO and CFO at Sidoti’s virtual small-cap conference
feed.summary: DHI Group, Inc. announced that its CEO, Art Zeile, and CFO, Greg Schippers, will participate in the virtual Sidoti Small Cap Conference on March 19, 2026. They are scheduled to present at 12:15 PM ET and will also hold one-on-one meetings with investors. A live webcast and replay of their presentation will be available for public access.

feed.title: KFC’s $20 wing deal targets college hoops watch parties
feed.summary: KFC has launched a limited-time "20 Wings for $20" deal starting March 12, 2026, targeting college basketball bracket season gatherings. This promotion offers nearly 50% savings compared to the average wing price and also marks the return of the cult-favorite Nashville Hot flavor. The deal is available through KFC.com and the app for participating stores, with a limit of one per transaction.

feed.title: Amcor wins UN approval for recycled containers
feed.summary: Amcor has achieved UN certification for its industrial containers made with 50% post-consumer recycled (PCR) content, allowing them to be used for storing and transporting hazardous materials. This approval ensures the containers meet strict safety standards while also helping manufacturers achieve sustainability goals and comply with European regulations. The containers are produced at Amcor's Pamplona facility and are suitable for various industries, including chemicals, pharmaceuticals, and automotive, with a high anticipated demand from the swimming pool chemicals sector.

feed.title: Mission Produce (NASDAQ: AVO) posts Q1 2026 loss while pushing $490M Calavo acquisition
feed.summary: Mission Produce reported a net loss of $0.7 million in Q1 2026, primarily due to $7.0 million in advisory costs related to its pending $490 million acquisition of Calavo Growers. Despite a 17% revenue decline to $278.6 million as avocado prices dropped, the company achieved 14% volume growth and an improved gross margin, leading to a 5% increase in adjusted EBITDA to $18.5 million. The Calavo merger is expected to close in Q3 2026, bringing anticipated annual synergies of at least $25 million and expanding Mission Produce's product portfolio.

feed.title: Strong Q1 2026 for Adobe (NASDAQ: ADBE) as long-time CEO plans transition
feed.summary: Adobe Inc. reported record first-quarter FY2026 results, with revenue reaching $6.40 billion, a 12% year-over-year increase, driven by subscription growth and a tripling of AI-first annualized recurring revenue. Alongside this strong financial performance, long-time CEO Shantanu Narayen announced his decision to transition from his role after a successor is appointed, though he will remain as Chair of the Board. The company provided optimistic Q2 FY2026 guidance and reaffirmed full-year targets, underscoring its continued focus on AI and profitable growth despite the upcoming leadership change.

feed.title: Quest Diagnostics Elects Timothy Wentworth, Former CEO of Walgreens Boots Alliance, Evernorth Health Services and Express Scripts, to Board of Directors
feed.summary: Quest Diagnostics has appointed Timothy Wentworth, former CEO of Walgreens Boots Alliance, Evernorth Health Services, and Express Scripts, to its board of directors. Wentworth brings extensive leadership experience from major healthcare organizations, including his role in restructuring Walgreens Boots Alliance and founding Evernorth Health Services. His expertise in health plan, employer, and consumer channels, alongside operational and HR management, is expected to be a valuable asset to Quest Diagnostics.

feed.title: INSULET recalls specific Omnipod 5 pods due to insulin leak risk
feed.summary: Insulet Corporation initiated a voluntary medical device correction for specific lots of Omnipod 5 Pods due to a manufacturing issue that could cause insulin leaks, potentially leading to high blood glucose levels. The company has received 18 reports of serious adverse events, including hospitalization and diabetic ketoacidosis, but no deaths. This correction affects approximately 1.5% of annual Omnipod 5 pod production globally and applies only to specific lots distributed in the United States, with the company having implemented updated manufacturing and quality controls.

feed.title: Corvus: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Corvus Pharmaceuticals Inc. (CRVS) reported a fourth-quarter loss of $12.3 million, or 15 cents per share. For the entire year, the company's loss totaled $15.3 million, equating to 53 cents per share. This financial data was generated with information from Zacks Investment Research.

feed.title: Ex-Walgreens CEO who ran $100B Express Scripts joins Quest board
feed.summary: Quest Diagnostics has elected Timothy Wentworth, former CEO of Walgreens Boots Alliance, Evernorth Health Services, and Express Scripts, to its board of directors, increasing the board to eleven members. Wentworth brings extensive experience in pharmacy, health plans, and operations, which Quest Diagnostics believes will support its diagnostic and health-services strategy. His past leadership roles include overseeing Express Scripts' growth to over $100 billion in revenues and restructuring Walgreens Boots Alliance.

feed.title: Insulet Initiates Voluntary Medical Device Correction for Certain Omnipod® 5 Pods in the U.S.
feed.summary: Insulet Corporation has initiated a voluntary Medical Device Correction for specific lots of Omnipod® 5 Pods in the U.S. due to a manufacturing issue that could cause insulin leakage and lead to high blood glucose levels or DKA. While 18 serious adverse events have been reported, no deaths have occurred, and only about 1.5% of annual Omnipod® 5 production is affected. Insulet has updated its manufacturing processes and customers can check their Pod lot numbers and request free replacements.

feed.title: Champions Oncology Reports Revenue of $16.6 Million
feed.summary: Champions Oncology, Inc. announced its financial results for the third quarter of fiscal 2026, reporting revenue of $16.6 million. The company is on track to deliver year-over-year revenue growth and positive adjusted EBITDA for the fiscal year, despite a slight decrease in total revenue compared to the prior year period due to the absence of a large data license transaction. Study service revenue increased by 32% year-over-year, reflecting strong study execution and conversion of previously booked work.
